title: RiskManager setVault allows permissionless vault replacement enabling protocol hijack
reason: `setVault` is one-time only (`InvalidInitialization` once set), so arbitrary replacement/takeover is incorrect; residual non-atomic initialization risk is already covered by F-006.

title: Hook delegatecall injection allows arbitrary code execution in vault context
reason: Delegatecall into hook is an explicit privileged extension model; hook addresses are role-gated (`SET_HOOK_ROLE`), so this is a trust assumption rather than a standalone vulnerability.

title: FeeManager updateState lacks reentrancy protection causing state inconsistency
reason: `updateState` performs no external calls and is keyed by `msg.sender` vault state, so reentrancy-based state corruption path is not supported.

title: Unchecked hook delegatecall return allows silent failures
reason: `Address.functionDelegateCall` reverts on failed delegatecall; hook interface returns no boolean, so the claimed silent-failure mode is not applicable.

title: Oracle timeout bypass allows stale price acceptance
reason: Suspicious reports are not forwarded to vault (`_handleReport` returns false), and final acceptance requires privileged `acceptReport`; stale price acceptance as described is unsupported.

title: TokenizedShareManager claimShares loop vulnerable to denial of service
reason: Duplicate of existing F-005 without material new evidence.

title: Fenwick tree index corruption in deposit cancellation uses wrong index
reason: Duplicate of existing F-002 without material new evidence.

title: Factory create allows blacklisted version after initial check bypass
reason: In-transaction blacklist race is not plausible under EVM atomic execution; state cannot change mid-transaction between check and proxy deployment.

title: RedeemQueue asset calculation uses division before subtraction causing precision loss
reason: Rounding dust from integer division is expected and does not show a concrete exploit path or realistic protocol-level harm.
